name: Baeckenoffa (Beef, Pork and Lamb Stew)
recipeIngredient: 500 g Boned shin of beef, trimmed (1 lb 2 oz)
500 g Boned shin of pork, trimmed
500 g Boned shoulder of lamb, trimmed
7 dl Dry white Alsace wine (3/4 qt)
1 1/4 kg Potatoes (2 3/4 lb), peeled and sliced (1/2 in thick)
800 g Leeks (1 3/4 lb) (trim the greenest parts, split the leeks lengthways, wash and dice)
80 g Goose fat or lard (3 oz)
1 bouquet garni, with plenty of thyme
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut each type of meat into 6 even pieces.
2. Place the meats in separate bowls and pour over the white wine. Cover and refrigerate for at least 12 hours.
3. Preheat the oven to 160°C (325°F).
4. In a flameproof earthenware casserole, heat the goose fat over low heat.
5. Add the onions and sweat them for several minutes.
6. Lay the leeks on top of the onions, then the potatoes.
7. Arrange the bouquet garni, peppercorns, and garlic separately on top, with one type to the left, one in the middle, and one on the right.
8. Pour over the remaining wine.
9. Season lightly with salt and add the chicken stock.
10. Place pieces of calf's foot on top.
11. Put the lid on the casserole.
12. Mix the flour with a little cold water to make a soft paste.
13. Spread the paste between the top of the casserole and the lid to create an airtight seal.
14. Cook in the low oven for 4 hours.
15. Present the casserole at the table as is, breaking the seal and lifting off the lid.
